The watch dates to 2001 , features the attractive black dial , and is unpolished , still retaining its original green case back sticker – it looks to have had minimal wear throughout its life so far. The watch comes with its original box, papers, booklets, tags and more , and was originally sold in Kuwait . #25 - The Rolex Explorer II 16570 w/ Box & Papers Collector's Set 2001 The History— The Example The Rolex Explorer II 16570 was introduced in 1989 and has become one of the great modern classic Rolex sports references – appreciated for its slim proportions, practical dual-time functionality and understated tool-watch character. This example dates to 2001 and features the attractive black dial , giving the watch a sharp, purposeful look on the wrist. It is also unpolished , still retaining its original green caseback sticker , suggesting minimal wear throughout its life so far. Powered by the Rolex calibre 3185 , the 16570 features an independently adjustable hour hand, allowing the wearer to track a second time zone with ease – a genuinely useful complication while retaining the clean, robust feel of a professional Rolex. This watch is accompanied by its original box, papers, booklets, tags and accessories , and was originally sold in Kuwait , adding an extra layer of provenance. A superb, honest and highly complete Explorer II – increasingly collectible, beautifully preserved and exactly the kind of example collectors look for. With an incredibly sleek profile thanks to its ultra-slim calibre 215 manual wind movement , this Calatrava is a very fine, elegant watch for both men and women. This example is in exceptional condition and comes with its original paperwork , whilst fitted to a superb custom Molequin strap to fit its original 18k gold Patek Philippe pin buckle. #27 - The Patek Philippe Calatrava 5022J Porcelain Dial Solid 18k Gold w/ Original Paperwork The History— The Example The Patek Philippe Calatrava has long been considered one of the greatest dress watches, known for its perfect proportions, restraint and understated elegance. This reference, the 5022J , is a particularly attractive modern classic iteration of the model, crafted in 18k solid yellow gold and featuring a beautiful white porcelain dial with elegant Breguet numerals and matching hands . Powered by the ultra-slim Patek Philippe calibre 215 manual wind movement, the watch has an incredibly sleek profile on the wrist, wearing with the sort of refinement expected from a Calatrava. At 33mm its classic proportions make it equally elegant for both men and women . This example is presented in exceptional condition and is accompanied by its original paperwork . It is currently fitted to a superb custom Molequin strap , paired with its original 18k gold Patek Philippe pin buckle . A beautifully executed and highly elegant Calatrava, combining classic Patek Philippe design with subtle, refined details throughout.
